title: Nark Suppressions Config (v1)
description: Schema for .nark/suppressions.json — marks scanner violations as known false positives or accepted-risk. See https://github.com/nark-sh/nark for the canonical IgnoreRule type.

$schema.description: Optional pointer to this schema. Including it enables IDE autocomplete + validation.

ignore.description: List of suppression rules. Each rule must match at least one of file/package/postconditionId and carry a meaningful reason (≥ 10 chars).

ignoreRule.title: IgnoreRule
ignoreRule.description: A single suppression entry. At least one of file, package, or postconditionId is required. The reason field is required (min 10 chars) so the rule is reviewable in the diff.

file.description: Optional glob pattern matched against the violation file (relative to the project root). Examples: "src/generated/**", "test/**/*.spec.ts". Omit to apply to all files.

package.description: npm package name the rule applies to. Use "*" to match any package. Examples: "axios", "@prisma/client", "*".

postconditionId.description: Postcondition ID from the nark profile. Use "*" to match any postcondition for the chosen package. Examples: "network-failure", "duplicate-key-error".

reason.description: Human-readable justification for the suppression. Required, minimum 10 characters — reviewers see this in the diff when the rule is added.
